name: Lemon Pepper Seasoning Mix
directions: Stir all the ingredients together and store in airtight jars.
Lemon pepper adds a piquant flavor when used on grilled meats.
Attach this to the Jar

name: Onion Soup Mix
directions: Combine in order listed.
Keep the mixture in jar at room temperature.
Makes 2 cups.
1/4 cup mix equals 1 envelope commercial soup mix TO USE: 1/4 cup mix to 4 cups boiling water.
Stir well until soup is dissolved.

similar.name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Emeril's Essence
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a mixing bowl.
Blend well.

name: Southwest Dry Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients in an airtight container, shake it up and use it up.

name: Gates & Son's KC BBQ Rub
directions: In a medium bowl, mix together all ingredients.
Store in a tightly sealed jar.

name: Italian Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in airtight container.

name: Herbal Shaker
directions: Mix together.
Put in an airtight container with a shaker lid.
Use in place of salt at the table.

name: Mrs. Dash Seasoning
directions: Mix well.
Keep dry.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Emeril's Southwest Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients thoroughly.
Store in an airtight container.

name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )
directions: Using an air tight container combine dry milk, cornstarch, onion flakes, pepper and chicken bouillon.
If desired also add basil and thyme.
Mix well.
When using the mix combine 1/3 cup of the mix with 1 1/4 cups of water in saucepan.
Cook and stir until thickened.

similar.name: Beef Vegetable and Barley Soup Starter Mix

name: Salt Free Taco Seasoning
directions: Put all ingredients in a labeled (Salt-free-taco) sandwich size zip lock plastic bag.
Seal and shake.
I keep my baggies of seasonings I make in an old bread tin, labeling the lip with a smudge free marker.
Then I can find the seasoning mix I need quickly.
When ready to use, shake bag before using.
This makes about 30 teaspoons Serving size is 1 teaspoon for use in recipes.
if calculating nutritional data purposes.

name: Barbecue Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in an airtight container until ready to use.

name: Greek Blend Spice Mix
directions: Mix together, store in a tightly capped jar.
Use: Sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon over each serving of chicken, lamb or shrimp before baking or grilling; or sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon over steamed vegetables.

This spice mix can also be used to make a yummy"Yoghurt and Herb" dressing, by adding a 1/4 teaspoon of the mix, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 Tablespoon olive oil and salt and pepper to 1 cup plain yoghurt.

name: Canadian Steak Rub
directions: In a bowl, combine all ingredients and stir until well blended.
Store mixture in an air-tight container.
To use: Rub the seasoning blend on a steak and grill, or use it to season beans, rices, grains or vegetables.

name: No-Salt Chili Powder
directions: Mix together well and store in a tightly capped glass jar.

name: Western Spice Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in a sealed container.
TIPS: Combine and grind briefly in a coffee grinder.
Substitute aniseed for fennel.
Use rounded tablespoons full when substituting seed for ground spice.
Instead of"tablespoons" use teaspoons to make enough to fill half a Schilling spice jar.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Homemade Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients together, and mix well.

name: Indian Spice Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients in an airtight spice jar.

name: Faux Montreal Steak Rub Seasoning Mix - Substitute

name: Steak Rub
directions: In a bowl, combine all ingredients and stir until well blended.
Store mixture in an air-tight container.
To use: Rub the seasoning blend on a steak and grill, or use it to season beans, rices, grains or vegetables.

name: Canada Rub
directions: In a bowl, combine all ingredients and stir until well blended.
Store mixture in an airtight container.
Use as a coarse-textured meat rub.
It also work well with mild-flavored fish.

name: Lemon Pepper Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Blend well.

name: Onion Paprika Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.

name: Homemade Curry Powder Mix

Combine with additional ingredients.
Makes about 2 tablespoons.

name: Shanghai Coastline Ethmix
directions: Grind to a fine powder.

name: Chili Powder
directions: The chiles should be toasted before grinding (one minute on a hot cast iron skillet or comal).
When the chiles are cool, break them into pieces and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee mill.
Add the rest of the ingredients and mix well.
Store in a jar.

name: Spicy Steak Rub
directions: Combine all of the ingredients and mix well.
To use: rub mixture on your choice of meat--this is really most excellent on steak.
Use more for more flavor, less if you don't want it as spicy.
Store in a small container with a tight fitting lid.

name: Chicken Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an air tight container.
Use as a seasoning for chicken, turkey, or any other poultry.

similar.name: Spice Mix for Pork or Chicken

name: Chinese 5 Spice
directions: Combine all ingredients in small airtight container.
Store in a cool, dry place.

name: Smokey Paste for Pulled Pork
directions: Mix dry ingredients together.
Add enough liquid smoke to make a paste.
Rub into meat and allow to marinate at least overnight.
Follow your favorite method for cooking pork!

name: Perfect Taco Seasoning

name: Homemade Pickling Spice
directions: Combine all ingredients, and store in a airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 10 days.

name: Mexican Fiesta Dip Mix - Gift Idea
directions: In a large bowl, combine the spices and store in an airtight container decorated with a pretty ribbon.

Attach this to the Jar: Mexican Fiesta Dip 3 tablespoons Mexican Fiesta Dip Mix 1 cup mayonnaise or lowfat mayonnaise 1 cup sour cream or lowfat yogurt In a medium mixing bowl combine the Dip Mix with the mayonnaise and sour cream.
Whisk the mixture until smooth.
Refrigerate for 2 to 4 hours.
Serve with tortilla chips or fresh vegetables.
Makes 2 cups.

name: Taco Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Can be used to season 2 flattened chicken breasts, sauteed in olive oil; plate, top with salsa, olives and cheddar cheese; microwave to melt cheese; top with sour cream.

name: Lemon Rosemary Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Shake before using to blend.

name: Seafood Herbs
directions: Combine all herbs and store in a tightly closed glass jar at room temperature.

name: Pasta Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature.

name: Beef Steak Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together well.

name: White Meat Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Mix well.
Store in air tight container.

name: Keen's Hot Mustard Dip
directions: Mix mustard, vinegar and water until you have a smooth paste.
Cover and let stand for 10 minutes.
Add the soy sauce.
Mix until well blended.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Ranch Dip & Dressing Mix Copycat

name: Smokey Steak Rub
directions: Mix spices, salt and peppers.
Rub over steak, chicken or fish.

name: Big Batch Ranch Dressing Mix
directions: Mix together all ingredients.
Store in a 1 cup canning jar or other airtight container.
When ready to make dressing, combine 1 cup of mayonnaise, 1 cup buttermilk, and 1 tablespoon dry dressing mix.
Whisk well.
Store in refrigerator.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Ranch Dip & Dressing Mix Copycat

name: Tex-mex Spice
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in a jar with a tight lid.

name: Homemade Sausage Seasoning Blend
directions: Blend all ingredients together in a spice grinder or mini-food processor (or you may also just stir together if you like some chunkier bits of fennel in your sausage).
Store in an airtight container until ready to use.
To use, mix with 1 pound ground meat.
Chill seasoned meat 3 hours before using.
Or you may also use this in any recipe calling for sausage seasoning.

name: Chili Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Add 3 tablespoons to each pound of meat when making chili.
Replace commercial chili powder in favorite recipes.
Use to season meat for tacos or taco salad.
Add to ground beef for grilled burgers or a meat loaf.

name: Herb Seasoning / Salt Substitute
directions: Combine all the seasonings.
pour into a salt shaker and use in place of salt or use as any herb saesoning would be.

name: Bryan's Jerk Rub: Jamiacan Style
directions: Mix ingredients together and rub on pork chops or chicken.
Grill or place in refridgerator until ready to grill.

name: Spicy New Orleans Grill Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients well.
Store in air tight container until needed.
Sprinkle the spices generously on the item and throughly rub it in before grilling.

name: Paul's Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix thoroughly, and store in a tightly sealed jar in a cool and dry place.

name: Southwestern Spice Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Enjoy.

name: Grainy French Style Mustard
directions: Crack the mustard seeds in a coffee or spice grinder - do not pulverize them just crack them.
Stir in enought vinegar to make a thick paste.
Combine mustard powder with the salt, stir in enough beer to make a thick paste.
Mix the two pastes together and add enough beer to make a smooth pasty consistency.
Place in a closed jar and store in the fridge.

name: Magic (barbecue) Dust
directions: Mix all together.
Put in a tightly covered container -- but put some in a shaker.
Shake on your meat and vegetables to taste.

name: Herb Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients in a small bowl.
Rub evenly over desired meat.

name: Chili Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients in a small bowl.
Rub evenly over desired meat.

name: Easy Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix together and use in place of a package of taco seasoning mix.

name: Taco Seasoning
directions: Put all of the above ingredients in a ziploc bag.
Seal.
To use, add 1 cup water and 1 packet mix to a pound of ground hamburger.
Simmer until thickened.
Makes taco meat or enchilada meat.

name: Ranch Dressing Mix
directions: Mix.
Store in jar or covered bowl.
Makes 1 cup mix.
To use:
Salad dressing: Add 1 tablespoon mix to 1 cup mayo and 1 cup buttermilk. Mix and use.
Dip: add 1 tablespoon mix to 8 ounces yogurt. Use with vegetables.

similar.name: Hidden Valley Ranch Dip & Dressing Mix Copycat

name: Tuscan White Beans
directions: Soak beans overnight in cold water to cover.
Drain; place in Dutch oven with garlic, onion, bay leaves and water to cover by 2 inches.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat.
Simmer covered 30-45 minutes, or until tender.
Drain; discard garlic, onion and bay leaves.
Toss with olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, salt and pepper.

name: Carrabba's Italian Dip Mix
directions: Grind together and put 1 tablespoon of mixture on plate.
Pour extra virgin olive oil over and mix together with mixture.
Dip warm french bread in the mixture.

name: Global Gourmet Herb Seasoning
directions: Grind all ingredients with a mortar and pestle or throw in your food processor until powdery.  Store tightly sealed.  The fresher the dried herbs, the longer the mix will keep its full flavor, but plan on about 3 months.

name: Poultry Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store tighty covered in a glass jar in a cool place, or store in the refrigerator.

name: Memphis Spice Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in airtight container for up to 2 weeks.
Use as desired.

name: Dry Rub for Barbecue
directions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Refried Beans
directions: Place beans in a big pot; add water to cover by 2 inches
Bring to a boil; boil for 2 minutes. Remove from the heat; cover and let stand for 1 hour.
Drain beans; discard liquid.
Return beans to pan; add water to cover. Add onion and garlic; bring to a boil. Cover and cook over low heat for 2 hours or until beans are very tender, adding water to keep covered if needed.
Discard onion and garlic.
Mash beans with a potato masher, leaving some beans whole.
Stir in cumin and hot pepper sauce.

name: Simple Dry Spice Barbecue Rub
directions: Mix all the ingredients together.
Rub generously on meat.
Marinate meat in the fridge if desired.
Store any leftover rub tightly covered in the refrigerator.

name: Emeril's Italian Blend
directions: Mix all of the ingredients together and put in an airtight container.

name: Make-Ahead Large Batch  Homemade Taco Seasoning
directions: Combine ingredients in a container with lid.
Shake vigorously.
Store in freezer until ready to use.

name: Black Magic Tri-Tip Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in a tightly sealed jar.

name: Gibson's Roasting Salt
directions: Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender and blend for 30 seconds. Transfer to a clean jelly jar or spice bottle, cover, label and store with your other spices.
For use on that great steak, coat steak with olive oil and sprinkle about 1/4 teaspoon of the roasting salt on each side just before cooking. Also great for grilled vegetables.
Enjoy!

name: Spicy Dry Rub
directions: In a small bowl stir everything together. Rub into shrimp, chicken or pork a few minutes before grilling.

name: Grill Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together well and store in an airtight container.
Use it for anything you're grilling or when you're looking for a grilled taste.
Note:  Corriander can be replaced with any herb you like.  This is the one I hear called for most often.  Experiment!

Another note:  This is a 3:1 ratio, for the most part.  Make any amount, just allow for 3 parts pepper, 1 part each of salt, onion powder, garlic powder and corriander, and 1/3 part crushed red pepper flakes.  Using tablespoons, this makes enough for 7 or 8 meals under normal usage.

name: Chicken Spice Bouquet
directions: Mix well and store in an air-tight jar for use when needed.
Sprinkle to taste on chicken dishes.
Add 1 tablespoon to a pot of chicken based soup.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Savory Steak Rub
directions: Combine all and rub into about 4 steaks.
Great for grilling or broiling.

name: Oriental Ramen Seasoning Copycat
directions: Mix all together, and store in an airtight container.

name: Minestrone Soup Gift Mix in a Jar
directions: Directions:
In a two-cup jar, layer from bottom in the order given, then seal the jar.
Include these instructions on your gift tag:
In a very large saucepan, combine 8-10 cups of water, a 28 oz can of crushed tomatoes and soup mix.
Add 2 chopped carrots, and 2-4 chopped potatoes (optional  2 cups chopped cabbage).
Bring to a boil; reduce heat and cover and simmer for one hour or until peas are tender -- .

similar.name: Beef Vegetable and Barley Soup Starter Mix
Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in small bowl until evenly distributed.  A plastic bag is great for this.
Tip onto 6 inch square sheet of aluminium foil and fold to make airtight, label and store in cool dry place.  Use within 6 months.

name: Texas Chili Powder - Bulk
directions: mix the spices together and store in a cool, dark location in an airtight container.
Final use requires 3-5 tbsps per pound of meat. 1 - 18oz can each of tomato sauce and chopped tomatoes, and 9oz water (half can to swish out the remaining tomato sauce).
Stew for at LEAST 45 minutes before serving.
Thicken with masa or cornstarch if necessary.
Makes about 2 quarts of chili.

similar.name: Make-Ahead Large Batch  Homemade Taco Seasoning

name: Turducken Seasoning
directions: Mix together and use as rub on poultry after rubbing the outide with butter or olive oil. Also used for the inside of each layer.

name: Chili Powder Mix
directions: In a medium bowl, toss all ingredients gently with spoon until well blended.
Store in an airtight container in cool, dark, dry place for up to 6 months.
Use in place of packaged chili powder.

name: Kittencal's Dry Onion Soup Mix Increaser and Flavor Inhancer
directions: Place all ingredients in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid; shake until well mixed.
Store the jar in the fridge.
Use 4-5 tablespoons in recipes for one package of dry onion soup.
Shake the jar before each use.

name: Boog Powell's Memphis Rubdown

name: Steak Spice
directions: Mix together.

name: Italian Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in airetight container.

name: West Indian Massala
directions: Toast all the seeds in a dry skillet, stirring well, until they begin popping.
Lower the heat and cook for an additional 5 minutes, taking care not to burn the seeds.
Cool the seeds and grind them all together finely in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle.
Add the turmeric and mix well if you wish to make a curry powder. Add the habanero powder for a hotter masala.
Store in a tightly closed container in your spice cabinet.

name: Dry Jerk Seasoning
directions: Mix together all ingredients.
Store the leftover seasoning in a tightly closed glass jar.  It will keep for over a month.

name: Fireworks Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl.
Transfer to an air tight container.  Store in a cool dry place.
Will keep approximately 6 months.

name: A South of the Border Spice Blend
directions: Place into a grinder, and blend for about 60 sec. Or you could just mix thoroughly with a spoon.
Store in a plastic or glass container.
Store in a cool place.

name: Spicy Three-Pepper Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.

Store in airtight container.

name: Texas Spice Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in sealed container.

name: Dolly Parton’s Cocoa Rub
directions: Mix together and use prior to grilling chicken and steaks.
Store the spice mixture in an airtight container; can be stored for up to 1 month.
Also is good stirred into chili and thick soups!

name: Blacken Spice Seasoning
directions: Mix all together and store with other spices.

name: Beef Vegetable and Barley Soup Starter Mix
directions: Combine the ingredients in a small bowl, and store in an airtight container.

name: Dry Rub
directions: Mix all.

name: Basic Mexican Seasoning Blend
directions: Mix all ingredients together and keep in a cool dry place.

name: Patrick's Chicken Rub
directions: Rub the chicken inside and out with this blend and allow it to sit for a few minutes prior to baking/roasting. It's good if you can get some under the skin too.
Not all the rub will stick to the chicken -- that's okay.
TIP: I also like to pour about 1/2 stick of melted butter all over the chicken, after it has been seasoned and is sitting in the roasting pot.

name: Gulf Spices (Ibzar)
directions: Combine all ingredients and store in a tightly covered container.

name: Medic Dave’s Super Seasoning
directions: Put in a Tupperware container.
Seal and shake well to mix.
Sprinkle, rub, or put in a shaker.

name: Smoking Brisket Rub
directions: The night before you plan to smoke, mix all ingredients.
Rub all over your "guest of honor," wrap in foil, plastic wrap or a large bag, and refrigerate overnight.
(I use plastic wrap and squeeze all the air out. This seems to work best for me).
30 minutes before you will start cooking, take meat from the fridge and set it out to come to room temperature.

name: Dry Onion Soup Mix
directions: Mix all together and store in container.
1/4 cup of this = 1 envelope.

similar.name: Kittencal's Dry Onion Soup Mix Increaser and Flavor Inhancer

name: Italian Seasoning
directions: Mix all together and store in container.

name: Mexican Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in container.

name: Jeri's Chili Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.  Use to flavor and thicken chili, using at least 2 tablespoons of mix per pound of meat.
Here's how I usually make chili with this mix:
1-2 lbs ground beef.
1 onion, chopped.
1 bell pepper chopped.
3 14-oz cans diced tomatoes.
3 cans beans (kidney, black, white, or a mix).
4-5 Tbsp chili mix.
1 can of beer.
Brown the meat, onion, and bell pepper.  Dump in everything else (don't drain the canned items) and bring to a boil.  Reduce heat and simmer for at least 30 minutes.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Dry Chimichurri Rub
directions: Whisk all ingredients in medium bowl.
Transfer to airtight container.
DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 month ahead.
Store at room temperature.

name: Spice Mix for Pork or Chicken
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in a jar.
If you cannot find smoked paprika, substitute with regular.

name: Neely's Seasoning
directions: Mix ingredients together.
Use as needed to season meat for barbecue.

name: Hidden Valley Ranch Dip & Dressing Mix Copycat
directions: Dump all ingredients into a blender or food processor and blend until completely powdered.
To Use: combine 1 tablespoons dry mix, 1 cup of may & 1 cup of buttermilk.

similar.name: Mexican Fiesta Dip Mix - Gift Idea

name: Chili Spice Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl.  To your taste, sprinkle mix on your favorite meat or veggies for an added punch of flavor. Makes about 1/3 cup.

***Tip-- for every 4 servings (1 lb boneless or 2 lb bone-in) meat, poultry or fish, mix 2 tablespoons Chili Spice Mix, 1 tbsp vegetable oil, and 1 clove crushed garlic.  Rub all over meat; let stand for 10 minutes.  (Make wet rub ahead: cover and refrigerate for up to 24 hours).

name: Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix together and add to ground beef aong with 1/2 to 3/4 cup water and cook until desired consistency.

name: Taco Seasoning (Salt Free)
directions: Place all spices in a zip top bag or jar with a tight fitting lid.  Shake well.  Use 1-2 T. per pound of meat.

name: Archangel's Egg Spice Recipe:
directions: Use 1 Pinch of mixture for each egg.

name: Caribbean Spice Rub
directions: Stir together all ingredients until well blended.

name: Rustic Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Store in an airtight container.
Use freely on meat and vegetables.

name: Neelys BBQ Dry Rub
directions: In small bowl combine all ingredients. Stir to combine.
Store in an airtight container.

name: "schmeer" for Roasts
directions: Combine spices in a small bowl. Add just enough water to make a very thick paste. Smear on beef roasts, pork roasts or chicken (whole or parts) before dry roasting.

name: Lulu's Jerk Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients. Store in air tight container.

name: Jim's Goodie's BBQ. Rub
directions: In a small bowl, mix together the brown sugar, paprika, mustard powder, onion powder, garlic powder, basil, bay leaves, coriander, savory, thyme, black pepper, white pepper, cumin, and salt.
Store in an air-tight jar at room temperature until ready to use.

name: Debbie's Bread Dipping Sauce
directions: Combine all the ingredients in a small food processor. Pulse 6 times untile all ingredients are combined.
To serve, combine about 2 tsp.spice blend to 4 T extra virgin olive oil and 1 teaspoons balsimic vinegar.
Dip bread pieces into mixture.

name: Moroccan Spice Rub (For Lamb & Other Meat)
directions: Mix the cumin, paprika, coriander, salt, pepper, cinnamon, allspice, cloves, and cayenne thoroughly.  Rub on the surface of the meat.  Rub the garlic over the spices and refrigerate overnight.  Without the garlic the spices will keep for months in a tightly covered, air-tight covered container.

name: Cindy's Mild Chili
directions: put pintos on to boil for about 3 hrs on medium or until tender than put kidney bean on to if you use dry if not wait until the pintos are in the last hour then put the can beans in diced 1 onion and bell pepper put them in 4 diced cerely stakles and boil gently 1 16 oz can of tomatoes or 3 chopped tomatoes  3 teaspoons of crushed red pepper flakes 2 tablespoons of oregno brown 1 pound of ground turkey drained and put it in let simmer for 1 1/2 hour then severe.

name: Everything Seasoning Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients and place in a small air tight container or spice jar.

name: Emeril's Spice Blend Recipes
directions: Combine all the ingredients (of your choice of spice blend) in a mixing bowl. Mix well.
Spice blends can be stored in an airtight container in your spice cabinet for up to 3 months.

name: Clone of a National Brand of Chili Powder
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Makes enough for one pot of chili.

name: Cream Soup Base
directions: Mix all the ingredients and store in an airtight container.  Makes 4-6 cups, depnding on serving size.

When you're ready to make the soup, put 2 cups water and the soup mix in a large saucepan and cook over medium heat, sitrring constantly, until thick.  Add the main ingredient of your choice and cook until done.  If the soup is too thick, add more water.  Season to taste.

similar.name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Caribbean Jerk Seasoning & Rub
directions: In jar with tight fitting lid, shake together all spices and seasonings. Store tightly covered at room temperature.

name: Magic Dust BBQ Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients and store in a tightly covered container.

name: Famous Jamaican Jerk Seasoning
directions: Combine onion powder, chives thyme, allspice, salt, sugar, black pepper, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, nutmeg and cinnamon. Use 2 tbsp per 4 servings.
Can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 months.

name: Salt-Free Chili Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in an airtight container.

name: North African Spices Seasoning Blend
directions: Mix all ingredients until well blended.

name: Fines Herbes Seasoning Mix Recipe, Make Your Own
directions: Combine the dried herbs. Place in a glass jar and seal tightly. Store in a cool, dark place up to 4 months.
Combine tarragon, chervil, chives, thyme, and parsley. Add fresh Fines Herbs at the end of the cooking process to preserve their flavor.

name: All Purpose BBQ Rub
directions: Combine the ingredients and store in a dry place.

name: Dry Rub for Sirloin Steaks
directions: Mix together and rub on sirloan steaks prior to grilling.

name: Homemade Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together in a small bowl.
Makes 3 tablespoons seasoning mix which is equal to 1/4 oz package of commercial seasoning mix.

name: N's Dry Rub
directions: Add all ingredients to a bowl and stir until combined. Keep in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

name: Dry Rub for Fish
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Use as you would any dry rub.

name: Mild Chili Powder
directions: Mix together,store in cool and dry place.

name: 6 Differents Dry Rubs
directions: For each rub:
Combine ingredients.
Store in covered container and keep in dry spot.
Can be sprinkled on meats and poultry just before cooking or several hours before.
Remember the longer time sprinkled on, the more intense the flavor.

name: All-Purpose BBQ Rub
directions: NOTE:3 more spices of your choice can be added in the ingredients. Zaar would not allow me to add that statement.
Combine all the ingredients in a bowl and you're good to go!

name: Southwest Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients and store in an airtight container.
Use to season all types of beef, chicken, pork, rice or potatoes.

name: Really Good Taco Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Place in an airtight container or a spice jar, and label.
Use in place of regular taco seasoning.
(I typically add about 2 T for each pound of ground beef or chopped chicken).

name: Dried English Mustard
directions: Sift all the ingredients together in a small bowl and pour into an airtight container.

name: Chinese Five Spice Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together well.   Store in a tighly sealed container.

name: Tuna and Beans
directions: Add chopped onion and lemon juice to a bowl; let steep while you make the salad.
Drain the beans and rinse them; arrange the beans in a bowl.
Drain the tuna and flake it into the beans.
Add the olive oil to the onion and lemon juice mixture; whisk to make a dressing; pour this over the tuna and beans; transfer to a serving dish.
Fork the salad through, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste, then scatter the parsley over the top.

name: Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
If making a larger batch, use two tablespoons of mix per taco packet.

name: Homemade Chili Powder
directions: Place all ingredients in a small container with a tight-fitting lid and shake to mix.
Store in a cook, dark place.

name: The Neely's Barbeque Seasoning
directions: Stir together all ingredients in a small bowl.
Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.
This seasoning will last up to 6 months.

name: The Perfect Yam / Sweet Potato Seasoning Spice Mix
directions: Mix the ingredients together well and store in an airtight container.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Asian Spice Mixture
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in airtight container.

name: Creole Blackening Rub
directions: Store in Airthight Container.
Rub well on both sides of meat of choice.
Cook as desired.

name: Steak Rub Spice Mixture
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in airtight container.
Rub well on both sides of meat.
Cook meat as desired.

name: Neely’s Barbecue Seasoning
directions: Stir together the ingredients in a small bowl. Stored in an airtight container in a cool, dry place, this seasoning will last for up to 6 months.
NOTES.

We never salt our Barbecue Sauce because of the sodium content in the ketchup and because other ingredients like onion powder and Worcestershire sauce provide so much flavor. Since our sauce is mostly used on grilled items (that are seasoned) and combined with other foods (like Barbecue Spaghetti and Molasses-Baked Beans), we don’t want to end up with food that is too salty. So we err on the side of slightly underseasoning this sauce (although believe me, no one ever says that it lacks flavor). If your taste buds yearn for a little more salt, you can season the sauce–at the end of the cooking time–as you please.

name: Adobo Seasoning - Salt Free
directions: Put all ingredients in an herb mill or use a morter and pestle to grind all together well.  Store in a jar.
Can add salt to taste if desired.

name: Taco Seasoning (For Feingold Stage 1)
directions: Combine all and keep in an airtight container. Makes 1 pkt.

name: Dry Rub
directions: Add all ingredients to a bowl and stir until combined.
Keep in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

name: Creole Dry Rub / Shake-On Seasoning
directions: Sift all the ingredients together in a bowl, and store in an airtight spice jar or container. Makes about 1/2 cup as posted, feel free to adjust up or down depending on how much you want, the proportions are all the same.

name: Mucho Macho Pepper Rub
directions: Mix all together.

name: The Pepper/Garlic Grind
directions: Mix and put in your favorite grinder.

name: Papa John's Pizza Seasoning
directions: Mix all thoroughly.

name: Yellow Split Pea Soup
directions: Put all the ingredients into the pressure cooker, making the chicken stock reach about 1 1/2 inches above the peas, depending on how thick u like the soup.
After the pressure cooker begins to whistle, let it boil for 15 minutes then shut it off.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Beef Vegetable and Barley Soup Starter Mix

name: Taco Seasoning Mix
directions: Blend all ingredients, using as much salt and black pepper as you like or none at all.
Store in a container with tight fitting lid, will keep indefinitely.

name: Taco Seasoning
directions: Mix it all together.

name: Adobo Seasoning
directions: In a bowl, stir together the salt, paprika, black pepper, onion powder, oregano, cumin, garlic powder, and chili powder.
Store in a sealed jar in a cool, dry place.

name: Cajun Spice Mix
directions: In a small mixing bowl, combine all ingredients.
Store mix in a clean 8-ounce Ball jar.

similar.name: A South of the Border Spice Blend

name: Chicken with Fluffy Dumplings
directions: Heat chicken broth and cooked chicken until bubbly.
Meanwhile, sift together dry ingredients.
Combine milk and oil; add to dry ingredients, stirring just until moistened.
Drop from tablespoons into bubbling stew.
Cover tightly; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat (don't lift cover) and simmer 12-15 minutes or until done.

similar.name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Dry Jerk Seasoning Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together.  Store leftovers in a tightly closed glass jar.  It will keep its pungency for over a month.
To convert this recipe for use as either a marinade (thinner) or a rub (thicker), add a mixture of 2 parts soy sauce to 1 part cider vinegar to jerk mix until desired consistency is reached.

name: Jamaican Dry Rub
directions: mix all ingredients in small bowl.
sprinkle over meat and rub inches Let meat sit in fridge for a couple hours before grilling.

name: Perfect Chili Seasoning Mix
directions: combine well and store in air tight container.

name: Lulu's Chipotle Taco Seasoning
directions: Combine and store in an airtight container.

name: Dk's Lone Star Dry Rub
directions: Mix spices thoroughly in a bowl.
You can store leftover rub in a tightly sealed jar in the refrigerator.

name: Salt-Free Blackened Seasoning
directions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl until mixed. Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.

name: Chili Powder Seasoning Blend
directions: Combine all ingredients.
store in an airtight container.

name: All Around Seasoning Mix
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients. Store in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

name: Shred's Cajun Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients in a resealable bag.  Makes enough for about 10 cuts of meat.

name: Spicy Paprika Herb Rub
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients. Store in a covered container. Rub over meat or poultry before cooking.

name: Chile Paste
directions: Bring 4 quarts of water to a boil. Cut stems off of chiles and remove seeds. Add chiles to boiling water for 10 minutes. Remove chiles and add to food processor. Grind, adding enough water to make a smooth paste.

similar.name: Clone of a National Brand of Chili Powder

name: Cream Soup Substitute
directions: Combine ingredients and store in an airtight container.
To make the equivalent of 1 can of soup, whisk 1/3 cup dry mix into 1 1/4 cup chicken, beef, or vegetable stock or bouillon.
Optional: saute 1/4 cup minced celery, onion, or mushrooms in 1 tbsp butter or olive oil until very soft. Add to stock before whisking in dry mix.

similar.name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Light Chili Powder
directions: Blend together and store in refrigerator for up to 6 months. For best results, grind cumin seed into powder.

name: Cream of Whatever Soup Mix
directions: Mix well and store in an air tight container.
When you need a cream of soup, for the equivalent of one can of condensed cream soup, mix 1/3 cup dry mix with 1 1/4 cup water.

Add bouillon of choice or other flavoring -- If something like celery, saute it in butter first then add.  Cheddar add 1/2 cup cheese and tsp dry mustard and replace water with milk.  Other bases, sauté and spice as desired.
Salt and pepper if needed.
Cook until thick.

similar.name: Condensed Cream of Soup Mix ( Homemade Substitute )

name: Prairie Dust
directions: Mix all ingredients and store in a tightly covered container.
Use on everything.

name: Mom's Stuffing for Turkey
directions: Cut bread into cubes or shred into pieces.  Toast lightly so it's not soggy.
Sauté onions until soft, not brown, and then add celery.
In a bowl, mix the vegetables with the bread, add enough liquid so that the bread is well moistened (but not soggy).
Add the beaten egg.  If it's a very large quantity, use two eggs.
Chop the herbs and add them to the bowl, plus add salt and pepper to taste.
Bake at 180 C (350 F) for 1 hour, or until crispy on the outside.

name: Cajun Herb Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.
Rub on meat just before cooking.

name: Bobby Flay's Barbecue Seasoning for Chips, Fries or Onion Rings
directions: Mix together all ingredients. Will keep in cool dark place for 1 month.

name: Garam Marsala
directions: Put the cumin, coriander, cardamom, peppercorns, cinnamon, and cloves in a dry heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Toast the spices, stirring occasionally, until they turn several shades darker and give off a sweet smoky aroma, about 10 minutes.  Do not raise the heat to quicken the process, or the spices will brown prematurely, leaving the insides undercooked.  Cool completely.

Working in batches if necessary transfers the mixture to a spice mill or coffee grinder and grind to a powder.  Stir in the nutmeg and saffron.  Use immediately or store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.

name: Jeremy's Spicy Rib Rub
directions: Combime all ingredients in a bowl and mix together. This recipe makes 14 ounces, so you can store any leftover in an airtight spice jar to make sure that the brown sugar doesn't clump.

name: Herbal Salt Substitute
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients. Store in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 1 year.

name: Perfect Creole Seasoning
directions: Put all spices in a bag and shake till well blended.

name: Cumin-Coriander Pork Rub
directions: Combine all the ingredients and store in an airtight container at room temperature.
TO USE:  Season both sides of your meat with 1-1/2 tsp of rub.  Grill or bake in 425F oven.  Oven cooking will take about 35 minutes.

name: Dry Steak Rub
directions: combine all in a bowl and mix well. store in a airtight container in a cool dark place and use as much as you wish!

name: Cajun Spicy Barbecue Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients and store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 month.
Rub desired amount onto meat of your choice and bbq.

name: Cajun Rub
directions: Mix the ingredients in a small bowl.

name: Caribbean Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl.

name: Toasted Cumin Rub
directions: In a medium skillet over medium-high heat, toast the cumin, mustard, and coriander seeds until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally to prevent scorching. Transfer the seeds to a spice mill, add the remaining ingredients, and pulse until finely ground.

name: Santa Fe Spice Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients in a small bowl.

name: Pork Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients in a small bowl.

name: Savory Southern Fried Seasoning
directions: 3/8 TEASPOON IS 1/4 TEASPOON PLUS ONE-HALF OF 1/4 TEASPOON. TO PREPARE A SINGLE BATCH OF FRIED CHICKEN OR TWO BATCHES PORK CHOPS OR CHICKEN FRIED STEAK, FOLLOW THIS RECIPE: 1 tablespoon fine sea salt, 1 tablespoon ground black pepper, 2 teaspoons celery salt, 2 teaspoons ground sage, 1 1/2 teaspoons paprika, 1 1/2 teaspoons powdered sugar, 3/4 teaspoon ground ginger, 1/2 teaspoon ground fennel, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon ground thyme, 1.4 teaspoon ground mace (or 1/8 teaspoon ground nutmeg), 1/4 teaspoon onion powder, 1/4 teaspoon ground rosemary, and 1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper. Shake mixture well in a resealable container with 1 3/4 cups all purpose flour and 1/4 cup cornstarch.
GRIND fennel seeds, rosemary leaf, and thyme, separately in a clean spice mill or coffee grinder, as finely as possible (for best grinding results, use 4 tablespoons of each).
MEASURE all recipe ingredients into a medium resealable container then seal.
SHAKE container well until thoroughly blended (if necessary, break up any clumps by pressing mixture with the back of a spoon, then re-seal and shake mixture again).
STORE tightly sealed in a cool, dry place away from light.

FOR FRIED CHICKEN BREADING: Measure 1 3/4 cups flour, 1/4 cup cornstarch and 1/3 level cup of this seasoning in a resealable container. Seal and shake well. If double-breading chicken, add an another 5 teaspoons flour and 1 teaspoon cornstarch to breading.

FOR CHICKEN FRIED STEAK OR PORK CHOP BREADING: Measure 7/8 cup of flour (3/4 cup, plus 2 tablespoons), 2 tablespoons cornstarch, and 8 teaspoons (2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons) of this seasoning into a resealable container. Seal and shake well. If double-breading chicken fried steak or pork chops, add an another 5 teaspoons flour and 1 teaspoon cornstarch to breading.
ENJOY!

name: Rustic Spice Rub (Bulk)
directions: Thoroughly mix all ingredients together, give it a taste, adjust seasonings to you own liking and store in air-tight container(s). Keep in cool, dark place (kitchen cabinet or pantry). Will stay fresh for several months.

*Note: If you want to divide the spice rub into smaller portions to give as gifts, use whatever size jars you choose (I use 4 oz baby food jars). If reusing old jars, thoroughly wash and remove the labels. Cut out a circle of cute, small printed material (I like small checkered print) larger than the lid of the jar (I use "pinking shears" to do this). Fill the jar and close tightly. Lay the material over the lid of the jar and secure with a rubber band around the neck of the jar. Tie a ribbon around the neck of the jar to cover the rubber band and make a bow (I like to use raffia so it truly gives it a "rustic" look). Add your own sticky label or attach a small gift tag to the ribbon to describe what is in the jar.
Enjoy!

name: AHa Herb Mix
directions: Combine all seasonings in a blender or food processor.
Blend ingredients just until they are ground.

name: Carrabba’s Bread Dipping Spices
directions: Place all ingredients except olive oil & lemon in a blender or food processor and chop until all ingredients are similar in size. Stir in the olive oil & lemon juice.
For Serving:
In a small dish, place 1 1/2 teaspoons spice mixture. Pour 3-4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil over top of the spice mixture. Serve with french bread for dipping.

name: Beef Dry Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl. Rub into beef of your choice. You can't upset too much.
reserve some rub to add to your mop if you make one.

name: Memphis Spice Rub
directions: In a small bowl, stir all the ingredients. Store in an airtight container for up to two weeks.
Great on baby back ribs, pork loin or tenderloin, pork butt, whole chicken, chicken breasts and thighs.

name: Rustic Rub
directions: Stir together all ingredients in a bowl until combined well.
Keeps in an airtight container at room temperature for 3 months.

name: Baby Back Ribs
directions: Hardwood chips or chunks of whole pecan wood should be used.

Prepare the grill for indirect heat: If you don’t have a smoker with an offset box, you can build a fire on one side of your grill and then put a piece of foil on the opposite side of the grill. This will create indirect heat.

Prepare the ribs: Remove the thin, papery membrane skin from the back of each rack of ribs by pulling it off in a sheet with your fingers, using the corner of a kitchen towel to gain a secure grip, or with pliers.
Prepare the rub: In a bowl, whisk together chile powder, salt, pepper, cumin, rosemary, thyme, garlic powder, and sugar.
Generously season ribs with the mixture, rubbing onto both sides of the rack.
Place the ribs, bone-side down, on foil to cook with lid closed. Cover the grill and keep the temperature of the grill at a constant 225-degrees. Cook the ribs for about 3 to 4 hours or until tender.

name: "Dorito" Popcorn Seasoning
directions: Combine all seasonings and blend in a coffee grinder, or blender, to make a fine powder.  Store in a seasoning jar.
Sprinkle seasoning over freshly popped popcorn (popped with oil - it won't stick to dry popcorn) and toss to coat.

name: Chipotle Taco Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
To use, mix 1 or 2 tablespoons with chicken, pork or beef.

name: MadCity Dale's CTC Chicken Tortillia Seasoning
directions: Mix all together thoroughly and store in well sealed glass jar.
Store in refrigerator or freezer to extend shelf life.
Optional;  Booyah spices #511313, High Cliff Chili spices #520724, MAdCity Dale's Cajun spices #452804, MAdCity Dale's Luv seasoning #520519.

name: CC Angus Chili Seasoning
directions: Measure accurately, blend well.
Store in air tight container, in refrigerator or freezer.

name: Suzy's All-Purpose Meat and Seafood Rub
directions: Mix all ingredients together well and store in an airtight shaker container.
To use on meat or seafood, just coat your meat/seafood with a little oil and liberally apply the rub.  Grill or cook as desired.

name: Emeril's Creole Seasoning Mix
directions: Combine all the ingredients thoroughly in a bowl.
Store in an airtight container away from light.
Use within 3 months.

name: Jeff's Dry Rub
directions: Mix together in a bowl.
Rub onto meat before grilling or smoking.

name: Homemade Chai
directions: Combine all ingredients. To prepare each serving, stir 1 1⁄2 tablespoons tea mix into 8 ounces hot brewed black tea.
Store in airtight container for up to 6 months.

name: Tasty Pizza Seasoning
directions: Mix all the spices together in a small to mid size container or seasoning container and shake it up to blend all the ingredients.
If you can't get ground rosemary use a mortar and pestle to grind the leaves or or finely chop them. A clean coffee grinder would also do the trick.

name: Taco Seasoning
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store up to a Year.

name: Classic Carolina BBQ Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients in a small bowl, mix well.
Use as a dry rub on beef, chicken, lamb or pork.
If  making ahead of time, store in an air tight container in a cool place for up to six months after preparation.

name: Turkish Spice Mix
directions: Stir spices together.
Store in an airtight jar.

name: Vegetable Broth Dry Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together, adjusting for your taste preferences.
Store in an air tight container.
May also be pulverized in a spice grinder for a finer texture.

name: Blackening Spice Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in bowl and stir to combine. Store in glass jar.

To use, sprinkle liberally on chicken or fish and cook in butter on high heat for blackening effect. Or not  --  I have used this blend to spice up chicken I roast in a crockpot. So it is very versatile!

name: Smoky Seasoning
directions: In a small bowl combine all ingredients.
Store in airtight container up to six months.

name: Emeril Lagasse Southwest Dry Rub
directions: Combine all ingredients thoroughly.
Can be stored in an air tight container for six months or so.

name: Savory Meat Rub
directions: Measure all ingredients into a small bowl.
Blend well. (Use a pestle and mortar to get a more melded flavor.).
If using celery salt, omit the salt listed.
Left over rub can be stored in a sealed container for up to a year.

name: Homemade Montreal Steak Spice
directions: Grind each individual ingredient to a similar coarse consistency. I use a small Magic Bullet but a blender or food processor should work too.
Blend all of the spices together well.
Store in air tight container. This recipe makes enough to fill a standard commercial spice shaker.

similar.name: Faux Montreal Steak Rub Seasoning Mix - Substitute

name: Ultimate Cajun Seasoning
directions: Mix spices (blend in spice grinder if desired).
Store in a well sealed jar and use as desired.

name: Authentic Taco Seasoning
directions: Combine all spices in an airtight container.  Store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

When making taco meat:  Cook meat in a skillet and drain, add 8 tsp of seasoning mix for every pound of cooked meat and add 1/2-3/4 cups water for every pound of meat.  Bring to a boil and stir, reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.

similar.name: Make-Ahead Large Batch  Homemade Taco Seasoning
